1Chr16:36.text: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting! Then all the people said, "Amen!" and praised the LORD.

Mt28:19.text: Go, therefore, and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the holy Spirit.

2Cor13:13.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ and the love of God and the fellowship of the holy Spirit be with all of you.

Lk2:14.text: Glory to God in the highest and on earth peace to those on whom his favor rests.

Is6:3.text: Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of hosts! All the earth is filled with his glory!

Jn1:29.text: Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world.

Mt6:9-13.text: This is how you are to pray: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name, your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as in heaven. Give us today our daily bread; and forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors; and do not subject us to the final test, but deliver us from the evil one.

Mk11:9.text: Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!

Gn14:19.text: Blessed be Abram by God Most High, the creator of heaven and earth.

Col1:16.text: For in him were created all things in heaven and on earth, the visible and the invisible, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers; all things were created through him and for him.

Lk1:35.text: The holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore the child to be born will be called holy, the Son of God.

Heb1:3.text: Who is the refulgence of his glory, the very imprint of his being, and who sustains all things by his mighty word.

Jn3:13.text: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who has come down from heaven, the Son of Man.

Mt1:18.text: Now this is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about. When his mother Mary was betrothed to Joseph, but before they lived together, she was found with child through the holy Spirit.

Jn19:16.text: Then he handed him over to them to be crucified.

1Cor15:3-4.text: For I handed on to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the scriptures; that he was buried; that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the scriptures.

Lk24:51.text: As he blessed them he parted from them and was taken up to heaven.

Col3:1.text: If then you were raised with Christ, seek what is above, where Christ is seated at the right hand of God.

2Tim4:1.text: I charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who will judge the living and the dead, and by his appearing and his kingly power.

Lk1:33.text: And he will rule over the house of Jacob forever, and of his kingdom there will be no end.

Jn14:27.text: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give it to you. Do not let your hearts be troubled or afraid.

Jn20:19.text: On the evening of that first day of the week, when the doors were locked, where the disciples were, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in their midst and said to them, 'Peace be with you.'

Rev19:9.text: Blessed are those who have been called to the wedding feast of the Lamb.

Mt8:8.text: Lord, I am not worthy to have you enter under my roof; only say the word and my servant will be healed.

Rom1:7.text: To all the beloved of God in Rome, called to be holy. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

Bar3:2.text: Hear, O Lord, have mercy, for we have sinned against you.

Ps85:8.text: Show us, LORD, your mercy; grant us your salvation.

Mk16:19.text: So then the Lord Jesus, after he spoke to them, was taken up into heaven and took his seat at the right hand of God.

Jn6:63.text: It is the spirit that gives life, while the flesh is of no avail. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.

Jn15:26.text: When the Advocate comes whom I will send you from the Father, the Spirit of truth that proceeds from the Father, he will testify to me.

2Pt1:21.text: For no prophecy ever came through human will; but rather human beings moved by the holy Spirit spoke under the influence of God.

Acts2:38.text: Peter said to them, 'Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins; and you will receive the gift of the holy Spirit.'

Mt26:26.text: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, said the blessing, broke it, and giving it to his disciples said, 'Take and eat; this is my body.'

Lk22:20.text: And likewise the cup after they had eaten, saying, 'This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which will be shed for you.'

Mt26:28.text: For this is my blood of the covenant, which will be shed on behalf of many for the forgiveness of sins.

1Cor11:24-25.text: 'This is my body that is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.' In the same way also the cup, after supper, saying, 'This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.'

Mal1:11.text: For from the rising of the sun, even to its setting, my name is great among the nations; And everywhere they bring sacrifice to my name, and a pure offering.

Ex24:8.text: Then he took the blood and sprinkled it on the people, saying, 'This is the blood of the covenant which the LORD has made with you according to all these words.'

1Cor11:26.text: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the death of the Lord until he comes.

Ti2:13.text: As we await the blessed hope, the appearance of the glory of the great God and of our savior Jesus Christ.
